snps.effect_template: HLA-DQ2.5 tag SNP. Major genetic risk factor for celiac disease (~95% of patients carry DQ2).
HLA-DQ8 tag SNP. Secondary celiac risk allele (found in remaining ~5% of patients).
IL2/IL21 protective variant reducing celiac disease risk through immune regulation.
CCR3 chemokine receptor variant affecting immune cell migration and celiac susceptibility.
SH2B3 adaptor protein variant affecting lymphocyte signaling in celiac disease.
RGS1 regulator of G-protein signaling variant modulating immune response and celiac risk.
TAGAP T-cell activation GTPase-activating protein variant affecting T-cell function in celiac disease.

recommendation_template: If you carry HLA-DQ2/DQ8 AND have symptoms (bloating, diarrhea, fatigue), consider tTG-IgA blood test. Genetic predisposition alone doesn't mean celiac — only ~3% of carriers develop it.

studies.title: Dense genotyping identifies and localizes multiple common and rare variant association signals in celiac disease
studies.finding: HLA-DQ2.5 (rs2187668) confers highest genetic risk for celiac disease (OR~7)

studies.title: Multiple common variants for celiac disease influencing immune gene expression
studies.finding: Identified multiple celiac susceptibility loci including IL2/IL21, CCR3, SH2B3, RGS1, and TAGAP immune-related variants
